title: Panorra API Go New Project Report

issues.display_title: Issue #382: Refactor Notifications Module to Use GORM Models
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 01/07/26

✅ Implementation: Notifications Module Refactoring

This document tracks the execution details for the Notifications module refactoring.

Phase 1: TokenNotification.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Notifications/TokenNotification.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Replaced Config.DB.Raw("INSERT...") and Config.DB.Exec("UPDATE...") with Models.NotificationsWeb and Models.PushNotification.
    • Used Config.DB.Create() for insertions and Config.DB.Model().Updates() for updates.
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 2: ReadAllNotification.go & ReadNotification.go

  • Target Files: src/Action/V1/Notifications/ReadAllNotification.go, src/Action/V1/Notifications/ReadNotification.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Replaced Config.DB.Exec("UPDATE ...") with Config.DB.Model(&Models.Notifications{}).Where(...).Updates(...).
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 3: PushNotification.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Notifications/PushNotification.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Replaced manual INSERT query for new notifications with Config.DB.Create(&Models.Notifications{}).
    • Included uuid.Parse() logic for parsing user UUIDs before insertion.
    • Removed raw untyped bindings where applicable to use structured models.
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Refactor task — based on docs/refactor_notifications.md

Background

The Notifications module currently handles operations (pushing notifications, managing tokens, marking as read) using manual SQL queries (tx.Raw, tx.Exec) and maps for retrieving data. Transitioning to GORM models will enforce type safety, avoid nil pointer panics on nullable database columns, and simplify SQL injection prevention.

Objective

Refactor the API endpoints within src/Action/V1/Notifications/ to utilize the existing GORM models (Models.Notifications, Models.NotificationsWeb, and Models.PushNotification).

APIs to Refactor

  • TokenNotification: Refactor insertion and update logic to use Config.DB.Create() and Config.DB.Model().Updates().
  • ReadAllNotification: Refactor update logic to use Config.DB.Model().Updates().
  • ReadNotification: Refactor update logic to use Config.DB.Model().Updates().
  • PushNotification: Refactor manual lookup and insert into standard GORM logic (Config.DB.Create()).

Acceptance Criteria

  • No raw SQL INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE queries remain in the refactored endpoints.
  • Explicitly generate and assign UUIDs using uuid.New() in Go before insertion to prevent zero-value constraint errors.
  • Untyped map[string]interface{} logic used for reading DB results is replaced with structured GORM model types.
  • The code successfully compiles (go build) without any unused imports.

issues.display_title: Issue #383: Refactor Search Module to Use GORM Models
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 01/07/26

✅ Implementation: Search Module Refactoring

This document tracks the execution details for the Search module refactoring.

Phase 1: AddSearchHistory.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Search/AddSearchHistory.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Replaced Config.DB.Table("history_search").Select("id") with Config.DB.Where(...).First(&Models.HistorySearch{}).
    • Replaced raw SQL INSERT INTO and UPDATE statements with Config.DB.Create() and Config.DB.Model().Updates().
    • Added explicit UUID parsing and generation before insertion.
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 2: DeleteSearchHistory.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Search/DeleteSearchHistory.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Replaced Config.DB.Table("history_search").Select("id").Scan() with structured queries using Models.HistorySearch.
    • Replaced raw SQL DELETE FROM execution block with dynamic Config.DB.Where(...).Delete(&Models.HistorySearch{}).
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Refactor task — based on docs/refactor_search.md

Background

The Search module currently handles operations (such as saving search history and deleting search history) using manual SQL queries (Config.DB.Exec) and raw table selection (Config.DB.Table). Transitioning to GORM models will enforce type safety, avoid nil pointer panics on nullable database columns, and simplify SQL injection prevention.

Objective

Refactor the API endpoints within src/Action/V1/Search/ to utilize the existing GORM models (Models.HistorySearch).

APIs to Refactor

  • AddSearchHistory: Refactor INSERT and UPDATE raw logic into standard GORM Config.DB.Create() and Config.DB.Model().Updates().
  • DeleteSearchHistory: Refactor DELETE FROM raw SQL query into standard GORM Config.DB.Delete().

Acceptance Criteria

  • No raw SQL INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE queries remain in the refactored endpoints.
  • Explicitly generate and assign UUIDs using uuid.New() in Go before insertion to prevent zero-value constraint errors.
  • Untyped map[string]interface{} logic used for reading DB results is replaced with structured GORM model types.
  • The code successfully compiles (go build) without any unused imports.

issues.display_title: Issue #384: Refactor Affiliate Module to Use GORM Models
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 01/07/26

✅ Implementation: Affiliate Module Refactoring

This document tracks the execution details for the Affiliate module refactoring.

Phase 1: AddAffiliate.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Affiliate/AddAffiliate.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Replaced Config.DB.Raw("INSERT INTO ...").Scan() with Models.Affiliate{} and Config.DB.Create().
    • Added explicit UUID parsing for user IDs and UUID generation with uuid.New() for the new affiliate.
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 2: UpdateAffiliate.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Affiliate/UpdateAffiliate.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Replaced Config.DB.Table("affiliate").Select("id").Where(...).Scan() with Config.DB.Where(...).First(&Models.Affiliate{}).
    • Replaced Config.DB.Exec("UPDATE affiliate ...") with Config.DB.Model(&Models.Affiliate{}).Updates(...).
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 3: DeleteAffiliate.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Affiliate/DeleteAffiliate.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Replaced Config.DB.Table("affiliate").Select("id").Where(...).Scan() with Config.DB.Where(...).First(&Models.Affiliate{}).
    • Replaced Config.DB.Exec("DELETE FROM affiliate ...") with Config.DB.Delete(&Models.Affiliate{}).
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Refactor task — based on docs/refactor_affiliate.md

Background

The Affiliate module currently handles operations (adding, updating, and deleting affiliates) using manual SQL queries (tx.Raw, tx.Exec) and maps/raw table selections for retrieving data. Transitioning to GORM models will enforce type safety, avoid nil pointer panics on nullable database columns, and simplify SQL injection prevention.

Objective

Refactor the API endpoints within src/Action/V1/Affiliate/ to utilize the existing GORM models (Models.Affiliate).

APIs to Refactor

  • AddAffiliate: Refactor insertion logic to use standard GORM Config.DB.Create().
  • UpdateAffiliate: Refactor raw ownership check and update logic to use Config.DB.First() and Config.DB.Model().Updates().
  • DeleteAffiliate: Refactor raw ownership check and deletion query to use Config.DB.First() and Config.DB.Delete().

Acceptance Criteria

  • No raw SQL INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE queries remain in the refactored endpoints.
  • Explicitly generate and assign UUIDs using uuid.New() in Go before insertion to prevent zero-value constraint errors.
  • Untyped map[string]interface{} logic used for reading DB results is replaced with structured GORM model types.
  • The code successfully compiles (go build) without any unused imports.
